name: Rhode Island SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Rhode Island's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to low-income individuals and families to purchase groceries. Administered by the RI Department of Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 185% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Rhode Island Medicaid
description: Rhode Island's Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, mental health, and substance abuse treatment for eligible residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL. Children: up to 261% FPL.

name: HealthSource RI
description: Rhode Island's state-based health insurance marketplace where individuals and families can shop for and enroll in qualified health insurance plans, and check eligibility for Medicaid or subsidies.
eligibilitySummary: Rhode Island residents seeking health insurance coverage
incomeLimitNotes: Subsidies available for households earning up to 400% FPL

name: Rhode Island Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to Rhode Island workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the RI Department of Labor and Training.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements

name: RIHousing (Rhode Island Housing)
description: Rhode Island's housing finance agency providing affordable housing resources including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, foreclosure prevention, and housing development financing.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income Rhode Island residents seeking housing assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 80% area median income

name: Rhode Island Legal Services (RILS)
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Rhode Islanders in areas including housing, family law, public benefits, immigration, disability rights, and consumer protection.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Rhode Island residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Rhode Island Center for Justice
description: Nonprofit legal advocacy organization providing free legal representation to low-income Rhode Islanders in civil rights, housing discrimination, education, and public benefits cases.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Rhode Island residents facing civil rights issues or systemic barriers

name: netWORKri
description: Rhode Island's workforce development system providing job search tools, career counseling, skills training, and employment services through local American Job Centers.
eligibilitySummary: All Rhode Island residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Rhode Island LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Helps low-income Rhode Island households pay heating bills and energy costs. Provides seasonal heating assistance and year-round crisis fuel assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Rhode Island households with heating costs
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: United Way of Rhode Island 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Rhode Islanders to local health and human services including food, housing, utilities, healthcare, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Rhode Island residents seeking community resource information
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Rhode Island Community Food Bank
description: The state's largest hunger relief organization, distributing food to over 160 member agencies across Rhode Island including food pantries, meal sites, shelters, and after-school programs.
eligibilitySummary: Any Rhode Island resident experiencing food insecurity

name: Rhode Island Office of Veterans Services
description: Provides services to Rhode Island veterans including benefits counseling, claims assistance, employment support, education benefits, and connections to VA healthcare and housing programs.
eligibilitySummary: Rhode Island veterans and their eligible dependents

name: BH Link (Behavioral Health Link)
description: Rhode Island's behavioral health triage center providing 24/7 crisis support for mental health and substance use emergencies. Walk-in and phone support available.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Rhode Island experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis

name: Rhode Island Child Care Assistance Program (CCAP)
description: Provides subsidized childcare to low-income working families in Rhode Island, helping parents maintain employment while their children receive quality childcare.
eligibilitySummary: Working families with children under 13 who meet income and work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 180% of federal poverty level at entry; 300% FPL to maintain

name: Rhode Island Works (TANF)
description: Cash assistance program for low-income families with children, providing monthly cash benefits along with employment services and supportive services to help families achieve economic independence.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children meeting work and income requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; varies by family size

name: Rhode Island WIC
description: Provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food benefits, and healthcare referrals for low-income pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Rhode Island Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income households including insulation, air sealing, heating system repairs, and energy audits to reduce utility costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: RIPTA (Rhode Island Public Transit Authority)
description: Statewide public bus system providing fixed-route and paratransit services throughout Rhode Island. Offers reduced fares for seniors, persons with disabilities, and Medicare cardholders.
eligibilitySummary: All Rhode Island residents; free rides for seniors 65+ and reduced fare programs available
hours: Varies by route; generally 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM

name: Rhode Island Office of Healthy Aging
description: Provides services for Rhode Island seniors including Meals on Wheels, senior centers, caregiver support, elder abuse prevention, and long-term care options counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Rhode Island residents age 60 and older, and their caregivers

name: Rhode Island Office of Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services to Rhode Islanders with disabilities including career counseling, job training, assistive technology, and job placement assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Rhode Island residents with physical, mental, or learning disabilities that impact employment

name: Crossroads Rhode Island
description: Rhode Island's largest homeless services organization providing emergency shelter, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and workforce development programs in Providence.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in Rhode Island

name: Providence Community Action Program (PROCAP)
description: Community action agency serving greater Providence with programs including fuel assistance, weatherization, Head Start, housing counseling, and financial literacy education.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of greater Providence area
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Dorcas International Institute of Rhode Island
description: Provides refugee resettlement, immigration legal services, English language education, citizenship preparation, workforce training, and cultural integration programs in Providence.
eligibilitySummary: Immigrants, refugees, and new Americans in Rhode Island
languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, Arabic, and others

name: Rhode Island Free Clinic
description: Provides free primary care, specialty care, dental care, and prescription assistance to uninsured Rhode Island adults who cannot afford healthcare.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured Rhode Island adults aged 19-64 earning below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level with no health insurance

name: Rhode Island Head Start
description: Comprehensive early childhood education program for low-income families providing education, health screenings, nutrition, and family support for children birth to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children birth to age 5, pregnant women, and children in foster care
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 100% of federal poverty level; categorical eligibility for certain groups

name: Rhode Island Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline operated by the Rhode Island Coalition Against Domestic Violence providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for survivors.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence in Rhode Island
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Rhode Island Department of Children, Youth and Families (DCYF)
description: State agency providing child protective services, foster care, adoption services, juvenile justice programs, and family support services for at-risk children and families.
eligibilitySummary: Children and families in need of child welfare services in Rhode Island

name: Rhode Island Emergency Management Agency (RIEMA)
description: Coordinates disaster preparedness, response, and recovery across Rhode Island. Manages emergency shelters and provides disaster assistance resources during emergencies.
eligibilitySummary: All Rhode Island residents affected by emergencies or disasters

name: Rhode Island Adult Education
description: Provides free adult basic education, GED preparation, ESL classes, and workforce readiness programs for Rhode Island adults seeking to improve their education and employment skills.
eligibilitySummary: Rhode Island adults age 16 and older not enrolled in school

name: Rhode Island Disability Law Center
description: The state's designated protection and advocacy agency providing free legal services to people with disabilities on issues including access to services, abuse and neglect, and civil rights.
eligibilitySummary: Rhode Island residents with disabilities facing rights violations or needing legal advocacy
